figures.key_information: Fabrication process showing (a) dropping inorganic polymer, PDMS stamp molding, UV exposure, peeling off mold and thermal curing, spin-coating diluted inorganic polymer on holed glass for sealing, UV and thermal curing; (b) SU-8 photoresist on silicon substrate, develop and strip SU-8, apply insert and stencil, RF etch and Cu/Pt sputter
figures.caption: Figure 2.  ( a ) Using PDMS materials for the manufacture of microreactors  [76] . ( b ) Wet etching  application in Pyrex glass with microchannels made of silicon layers  [77] .

figures.flow_path: two-phase flow with Phase A and Phase B streams
figures.mixer_type: multiple types shown: Baker-mixer, Smale-mixer, Helical-mixer
figures.key_information: Shows three different passive mixer designs (Baker, Smale, and Helical) with two-phase flow visualization and concentration gradients indicated by color mapping
figures.caption: Figure 4.  Schematic and flow pattern of microreactor flow systems for ( a ) laminar flow [114], ( b ) slug  flow [126], and ( c ) integrated flow [124].

figures.flow_path: Various configurations shown including parallel, series, and network arrangements
figures.reactor_geometry: Multiple geometries shown: capillary, plate, laminar, parallel, series, and network
figures.key_information: Schematic showing different microreactor configurations with Stream A and Stream B inputs, including various reactor geometries and connection patterns
figures.caption: Figure 5.  Microreactor types: ( a ) typical reactor [136], ( b ) parallel and series reactors [43], and ( c )  network reactor [43].   Images from Šalić, A.; Tušek, A.; Zelić, B.: Application of microreactors in  medicine and biomedicine.  Journal of Applied Biomedicine  2012, 10, 137–153.

figure_descriptions: Fabrication process showing PDMS stamp molding and silicon substrate processing
Shows three different passive mixer designs with two-phase flow visualization
Schematic showing different microreactor configurations with various geometries

notes: This is a review paper on microreactor fabrication and design, not a specific chemistry study. No batch-to-flow translation or optimization data available. Limited quantitative process parameters extracted. Figures focus on reactor design and manufacturing methods rather than chemical processes.
